headline: GOLD FORECAST ROUNDUP: Analysts Revise Targets to $4,500–$7,000 After Record Q1 — Where Do Banks See Gold Going?
snippet: After gold set a Q1 all-time high near $5,595/oz, major bank and analyst gold forecasts for 2026 have been revised sharply upward — with targets ranging from $4,500 to $7,000+. Structural central bank buying, dollar weakness, and safe-haven demand are cited as durable supports.

headline: RECORD VEIN GRADE: Americas G&S Hits 4,896 g/t Ag at Galena — 10 New High-Grade Veins, 64,000m Program Launched
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver discovered 10 new high-grade veins at its Galena Complex in Idaho — including 4,896 g/t Ag and 3.95% Cu over 1.3m. The company is launching its largest-ever exploration campaign: 64,000 metres across Galena and Cosalá in 2026.

headline: US DOE Commits $2.7B to Domestic Uranium Enrichment — Section 232 Designates Uranium a National Security Asset
snippet: The U.S. Department of Energy committed $2.7 billion over the next decade to expand domestic uranium enrichment capacity under the Section 232 national security framework. The move aims to cut reliance on foreign enrichment as spot uranium trades near $100/lb.

headline: Goldman Sachs: COPPER to Ease From Record Highs — Long-Term Supply Deficit Remains Structurally Intact
snippet: Goldman Sachs forecasts copper prices will pull back from current record levels above $13,000/tonne as near-term demand softens, but maintains a bullish long-term view based on an irreversible structural supply deficit driven by the energy transition. A contrarian note to the current momentum trade.

headline: NOT MP Materials, Not USAR — Motley Fool Analyst Reveals the Rare Earth Stock That Finally Met the Bar
snippet: A Motley Fool analyst published their buy case for a rare earth stock that bypasses both MP Materials and USA Rare Earth, targeting a different part of the domestic supply chain as the better risk/reward play amid China's export controls and Trump's Project Vault initiative.

headline: Energy Storage BOOM Resets Lithium Demand Outlook — Battery Storage to Drive Record Consumption in 2026
snippet: A boom in grid-scale battery storage has materially strengthened the demand outlook for lithium in 2026, with China's power sector reforms driving stronger-than-expected consumption in H2 2025. Structural deficits are projected to grow for most battery metals over the next decade.

headline: Gold Fields CEO: OPEN TO More M&A — High Gold Prices Create 'Best Acquisition Currency in a Decade'
snippet: Gold Fields CEO Mike Fraser told investors the company is actively evaluating M&A opportunities after strong free cash flow generation at elevated gold prices. With spot gold near $4,750 and producer balance sheets at historic strength, strategic acquisitions of mid-tier and junior assets are firmly on the table.

headline: GoldSeek SWOT: China Silver Imports HIT 790+ Tonnes in Early 2026 — Solar Manufacturing Surge Tightens Global Supply
snippet: GoldSeek's weekly Gold SWOT analysis highlights China importing more than 790 tonnes of silver in early 2026, driven largely by record solar panel manufacturing. Tight silver inventories and structural demand continue to support prices despite broader macroeconomic headwinds.

headline: Gold Miners at Q2 Start: WINNERS and LOSERS as Equities Finally Chase $4,700+ Gold After Years of Lag
snippet: A Q2 2026 gold equity scorecard tracks which producers and developers are outperforming vs. underperforming as the sector attempts to close its multi-year valuation discount. Generalist funds remain underweight, and record cash flows are beginning to attract incremental capital to the space.

headline: Junior Miners READY TO RUN? INN Identifies Top 5 TSX-V Gold Stocks Best Positioned for $5,000+ Gold
snippet: Investing News Network identifies five TSX-V junior gold stocks best positioned to capitalise on sustained high gold prices. With gold up 46% year-over-year and juniors historically lagging in the early-to-mid bull cycle, specific names with advanced projects and tight share structures stand out.

headline: Sprott Money: WHY Gold and Silver Miners Lack Reserves — The Supply Crisis Hiding Inside the Bull Market
snippet: Sprott Money's analysis argues that decades of underinvestment in exploration have left major gold and silver miners with historically thin reserve pipelines — a structural supply constraint that cuts both ways: bullish for gold prices long-term, but bearish for production growth at existing companies.
